audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for not receiving grace in vain, servant hardships, paradoxes, open heart, unequal yoke, temple of living Divine.

verses.restored: And working together, we also appeal that you not receive the grace of the Divine into emptiness.
For he says, In an acceptable season I listened to you, and in a day of salvation/healing I helped you. Look, now is the well-accepted season; look, now is the day of salvation/healing.
Giving no stumbling in anything, so that the service/ministry may not be faulted,
but in everything commending ourselves as servants of the Divine: in much endurance, in pressures, in necessities, in constraints,
in beatings, in prisons, in riots, in labors, in sleepless nights, in fastings,
in purity, in knowledge, in patience, in kindness, in holy Spirit, in unhypocritical love,
in word of truth, in power of the Divine, through the weapons of righteousness/justice on the right and left,
through glory and dishonor, through harmful report and good report; as deceivers and true,
as unknown and well known, as dying and look—we live; as disciplined and not killed,
as grieving yet always rejoicing, as poor yet enriching many, as having nothing and possessing all things.
Our mouth has opened to you, Corinthians; our heart has been widened.
You are not constrained in us, but you are constrained in your own inward parts.
In the same exchange, as to children I speak, be widened also.
Do not become unequally yoked with untrusting ones; for what partnership have righteousness/justice and lawlessness? Or what fellowship has light with darkness?
What harmony has Messiah with Belial? Or what share has a trusting one with an untrusting one?

What agreement has a temple of the Divine with idols? For we are temple of living Divine, just as the Divine said: I will dwell in them and walk among them, and I will be their Divine, and they will be my people.
Therefore come out from their midst and be separated, says the Lord, and touch nothing unclean; and I will receive you,
and I will be Father to you, and you will be sons and daughters to me, says Lord Almighty.

translation_notes: The unequal-yoke section should be handled as covenant/participation discernment, not contempt for outsiders. It follows the open-heart appeal and must not negate Paul's reconciliation ministry.
The hardship catalog presents weakness and pressure as signs of servant integrity, not failure.
